facts.text: Theranos Inc. (/ˈθɛr.ən.oʊs/) was an American privately held corporation that was touted as a breakthrough health technology company.

Theranos was a health technology company founded in 2003 by Elizabeth Holmes, who aimed to revolutionize blood testing by creating devices that could perform a wide array of tests using just a few drops of blood at significantly lower costs. The company quickly gained attention and investment, achieving a peak valuation of over $9 billion by 2015, attracting high-profile investors such as Rupert Murdoch and Betsy DeVos. However, in 2015, investigative reports began to challenge Theranos's claims

Theranos Inc. was a consumer healthcare technology startup that closed down in 2018 after the company's founder and former CEO Elizabeth Holmes and former president Ramesh "Sunny" Balwani were indicted by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). * Theranos founder and CEO Elizabeth Holmes claimed that the company had developed a new technology to test blood, but these claims were later discovered to be fraudulent. Theranos was a privately-held healthcare technology company founded in 2
